knowledge.title: Knowledge Base
knowledge.subtitle: Materials and articles on sports medicine

article1.title: Modern methods of sports injury diagnosis
article1.desc: Overview of current methods for diagnosis and treatment of injuries in athletes
article1.content:
Sports injuries require a special approach to diagnosis.

Modern methods include:

  • MRI and CT diagnostics
  • Ultrasound examination
  • Arthroscopy
  • Functional testing

Early and accurate diagnosis reduces recovery time and prevents complications.

article2.title: Cardiological screening of athletes
article2.content:
Regular cardiac screening is necessary for all athletes.

Recommended examination scope:

  • Resting and stress ECG
  • Echocardiography
  • Holter monitoring
  • Blood test for cardiac markers

Examination frequency - at least once a year for professional athletes.

article3.title: Principles of post-injury rehabilitation
article3.desc: Stages of recovery and return to training
article3.content:
Proper rehabilitation is the key to successful return to sports.

Main stages:

  1. Immobilization and rest
  2. Restoring range of motion
  3. Muscle strengthening
  4. Functional training
  5. Return to sports activity

Each stage should be monitored by a doctor.

article4.title: Fundamentals of sports nutrition
article4.desc: Principles of diet composition for athletes
article4.content:
Proper nutrition provides energy and accelerates recovery.

Key principles:

  • Balance of proteins, fats and carbohydrates
  • Fractional meals 5-6 times a day
  • Adequate water intake
  • Consideration of training cycle period

Individual approach to nutrition improves athletic performance.

article5.title: Prevention of recurrent injuries
article5.desc: Methods to prevent recurrence of sports injuries
article5.content:
Prevention of recurrent injuries includes a comprehensive approach.

Key measures:

  • Proper warm-up before exercise
  • Strengthening stabilizer muscles
  • Use of protective equipment
  • Proper exercise technique
  • Gradual increase in loads

Regular medical supervision helps identify risks at early stages.

help.title: Help & Support
help.subtitle: Frequently asked questions and guides
help.a1: Select the desired test from the list on the main page and click "Start".
help.a2: Test results are available in the "Statistics" section.
help.a3: Go to the "Profile" section and click the "Edit" button.
help.a4: Use the contacts below or write to support email.
